description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu

Rank: 4

Chakra Cost: 6

Learn DC: 17 | Successes: 3

Perform: 5 | Perform DC: 17

Components: H

*Base Will save is the bonus gained from your class(es).

This signature technique of the Yamanaka Clan allows you to send out your mind to control another creature's body. The technique affects the creature within the area that is closest to you. If that creature does not identify the technique or if it is unable to move sufficiently (because it is pinned or paralyzed, for example), it cannot make a Reflex save to avoid it. If the creature succeeds at the Reflex save, it will take 1d6+1 rounds for your mind to return to your body (see below).

If the creature is hit, it may make a Will save to resist the technique only if it is at least 5 levels higher than you. If that save succeeds, you take 1d6 damage for every 5 points by which they beat the save DC. If the technique succeeds, your body falls unconscious while your mind takes over the target. Once your mind returns, you immediately wake up. While possessing a body, you lose all your physical attributes and gain those of the target's (except HP), which amounts to gaining the following:

- The target's creature type, natural armor, natural weapons, movement speed and other physical characteristics of its body.
- The target's Str, Dex and Con scores, which now modify your statistics and die rolls (except for HP).
- The target's Chakra Signature and current Chakra Pool, as well as its Elemental Affinities.
- The target's Extraordinary abilities and qualities (if any), and any Supernatural or Spell-like abilities that were already active, for as long as their durations remain. Strength and Speed ranks are inaccessible and inactive.
- You lose all of the statistics and abilities listed above that your own body possesses. You retain your supernatural and spell-like abilities.

Note that your new statistics may make you unable to use some of the feats or special abilities, such as if an ability score does not meet its prerequisite.

There is no limit to how far you may travel with the possessed body, and if you take an action which the subject is strongly against, it can attempt a Will save to end the technique and regain control of its body. While possessing the body, treat your HP as a separate pool from the subject's own HP: the subject's body will suffer damage as normal, and half of such damage is also dealt to your own HP. If your real body is damaged, reduce your HP accordingly.

When your real body is reduced to 0 HP or below, the technique ends and you fall unconscious after returning to your real body. If your real body dies, you die. If the possessed body is reduced to 0 HP or below, you act as normal, though if the possessed body dies, you also die. All conditions and afflictions suffered remain with the possessed body, but any Int, Wis or Cha damage sustained while possessing a creature is applied to you and not to the possessed body (and, as such, remains on you even after the technique ends).

Mastery: Each step reduces the time it takes for your mind to return to your body on a miss by 1 round, down to a minimum of 2 rounds.

The third step increases the duration to 5r/level. The fifth step increases the duration to 1m/level.
